disclaimer: Fiction, atmosphere, and deliberate inference. Does not override parish proof or interpretation.authority_note in the lineage file. Campti is the narrative stage; GRP-* ids parallel genealogy for prompts and scenes.

setting.geography: Campti (Natchitoches Parish), Red River, Isle Brevelle, upper river memory vs present-day town — your novel's ground truth is emotional and political, not a single map coordinate.
setting.story_function: Every Grappé generation eventually answers to this place: who holds land, who names children, who speaks for the river when the empire changes.
tone_notes: Post-Purchase paperwork rubbing against oral tenure
River as character — floods, fords, testimony

branch_map.summary: Three layers: founding (Alexis+Louise), Rosette generation (Francois+Rosette), optional Model B subgraph (Jeanne + St Denis parents GRP-0330–0332) parallel to Rosette labels, then forks — Pelagie/Perot pivot at Emmanuel Lucas GRP-0320 vs Jacques/De La Cerda parallel. Forward: LeBrun / Dorthlon / Fee Trichel still need parish proof. Marie Pelagie 1766 is Emmanuel's mother — not Pelagie b.1790.
unresolved_or_noisy: Son Jacques Grappe + spouse collision with Marie Rosa (GRP-0306)
Exact birth years for several Jacques-branch children
Whether Emmanuel Lucas maps to GRP-0214 Manuel or a distinct person
Pelagie name collision: Marie Pelagie 1766 (GRP-0042) vs Pelagie 1790 (GRP-0052)
Rosette (GRP-0041) vs Marie Jeanne (GRP-0330) as Pelagie's mother — Model A vs B; parish merge before narrative canon

documents.title: Deposition of Francis Grappe before John Sibley JP (Red River French settlements)
documents.author: John Sibley (recorder); Francois/Francis Grappe (deponent)
extracted_facts: Deponent 'of Campte' (Campti), age 57 in Sept 1805 → birth ~1748.
Born near ancient Caddo village on Red River; 'upwards of five hundred miles above Natchitoches' by river course.
Lived there until age sixteen or seventeen; parents had lived there years before his birth.
Father commandant for years; succeeded by M. Gloso; post abandoned after Louisiana cession to Spain (1762).
Father built small fort by governor's order: two small cannon, flagstaff, French flag occasionally hoisted.
~30 years French occupation as military post + family settlement; agriculture like lower Louisiana.
Mill stones: deponent brought them down in 1778 to Opelousas; said Company had brought them to Caddo country.
Also describes Dout on Sabine, Yattasee Point / Bayou Pierre area, Spanish jurisdiction, Verge exclusive Indian trade — second paragraph.
inferred: Witness is credible to US as intelligence asset: trade geography + French residue after 1762.
Childhood memory reaches back before 1762 abandonment — good for timeline of father's role.
Mother absent from text: room for Rosette as silent or off-record in fiction (do not assert as fact).
between_the_lines: Sibley's mission: new state inventorying land, improvements, and whether locals are 'hostile' — testimony is also surveillance.
Naming 'the Company' as pre-birth trader network — corporate ghost in family memory.
Moving mill stones 1778: heavy logistics as story image of displacement and reuse.
fiction_hooks: Swearing-in room 1805: smell of ink, September heat, age 57 body remembering cold at the upper post.
Two cannon that never quite equal 'fifteen soldiers' on paper.
Flag 'occasionally' hoisted — when, and for whom?

documents.title: Testimony by Francois Grappe about the French along the Red River in New Orleans Territory
documents.author: Robin Cole-Jett
extracted_facts: Context: Dr. John Sibley Indian agent + JP Natchitoches; summer 1805 counts; autumn task to interview prior inhabitants for US land use.
1729 map reference: Yatachez / Nasonis villages along R. Rouge; Ruderman map detail.
Nassonites / Caddoquis: mound region, long continuity; de la Harpe 1719 trading posts; Harpe post location still debated (Bowie County TX conjecture, right bank).
Yattasee Point: displaced peoples, Bayou Pierre / Red near Shreveport; vacherie; African/Native Creoles, Panis, Canechi freed under royal decree context.
Fort le Dout (Sabine / Lake Fork area): Handbook of Texas; Woldert site possibility.
Historiographic split: McConnell '500 miles' twin villages OK-TX vs Cole-Jett Lamar County Fort St. Louis de Carolette reading — not resolved in article.
inferred: Upper Red is a palimpsest: French company posts, Caddo towns, later American grid — good for layered haunt in fiction.
Yattasee pocket as refuge ecology matches themes of Rosette-line liminality (inference only).
between_the_lines: Article's job is partly to correct Texas canon — your novel can mirror that: contested memory vs map.
Indigenous nations named but testimony centered on French infrastructure — whose words get preserved?
fiction_hooks: Map table: finger tracing R. Rouge while deponent speaks '500 miles'.
Fort Dout as unseen ruin — 'remains of buildings and works now to be seen' from deposition echoed in Cole-Jett.
Yattasee ad-hoc village: displaced stacking on displaced — extra plot pressure for mixed-lineage household stories.

documents.title: User batch: Jacques Grappe branch vs Pelagie/Perot fork
documents.author: Project notes
extracted_facts: Jacques Grappe b. 20 Sep 1763 Natchitoches; d. Oct 1848 Claiborne Parish — son of Francois+Rosette (parallel branch).
Marie Rosa De La Cerda ca. 1800 Nacogdoches — ca. 1853; spouse of Jacques.
Children include Francois (s/o Jacques), Gabriel, Tebourse Jacques, Severin, Margil/Magee, Jacques (son), Marie Louise, Marie Jeanne.
Francois (s/o Jacques) married Marie Julienne Trichel; witness Eugene D'Ortolon — Trichel/D'Ortolon network crosses this branch.
Direct-path candidate remains Pelagie/Perot/Emmanuel Lucas line — not replaced by Jacques branch.
inferred: Jacques branch shows Natchitoches/Campti → Claiborne/Bienville radiation — good for novel geography even if not your DNA line.
Two Francois Grappe labels (Sr. vs son of Jacques) are a permanent disambiguation problem in prompts.
between_the_lines: Compiled trees duplicate 'Jacques' as child with same spouse as father — classic GEDCOM collision.
Trichel appears on the 'wrong' branch first — history is networked, not linear.
fiction_hooks: Two brothers named Francois in living memory — which one the courthouse clerk meant.
Claiborne burial weather vs Campti porch — same family, different county silence.

documents.title: Screenshot chain: Francois+Rosette → Marie Pelagie+Perot → Emmanuel Lucas
documents.author: Project / Ancestry screenshots
extracted_facts: Francois Baptiste Touline Grappe + Marie Louise Rosette (dit Souris Jeanne, Dupre-associated) consistent across panels.
Marie Pelagie Grappe b.1766 + Jean Chrysostome Perot → Emmanuel Lucas Grappe Perot b.1801 d.1860 with dual Grappe/Perot naming.
Pelagie b.1790 (GRP-0052) appears in same household timeline as sibling — not Emmanuel's mother (chronology).
Trichel names (e.g. Pierre Andre Athanase Trichel) appear in related streams — network crosses before later Trichel marriages.
inferred: Rosette generation is the documented enslaved/free Creole maternal pivot — not a 'missing' branch.
Emmanuel is the identity-transition generation for surname and legal status modeling in fiction.
between_the_lines: Screenshots prove structure across UI, not a single parish PDF — next step is citation-grade extraction.
Mixed-status sibling labels in trees need per-person parish support.
fiction_hooks: Two Pelagies in one scroll — the UI lies unless you read birth years.
Dupre string — who held the pen vs who held the child.

documents.title: Screenshot chain: Marie Jeanne; parents Francois (St Denis) + Fanny
documents.author: Project / Ancestry screenshots
extracted_facts: Some panels name Pelagie's mother as Marie Jeanne (enslaved) with father Francois Baptiste Touline Grappe.
Jeanne's parents named as Francois (St Denis slave) and Fanny — extends documented enslaved line before Rosette-only labels in other imports.
1779 marriage/property contract cited by user as status document (assets, Trichel/Fontenot network) — needs full archive citation in lineage file.
inferred: Model A (Rosette as mother in JSON) vs Model B (Jeanne + St Denis grandparents) must merge in parish acts — fiction can hold both until proof.
'St Denis' may index Louis Juchereau de St Denis colonial networks — not automatic proof of estate or individual.
between_the_lines: Same bloodline can carry multiple colonial name layers — silence is not absence.
Property contracts list people as assets — different evidentiary weight than christening margins.
fiction_hooks: A mother known under three names in three documents — which one the church used when Pelagie married.
Fanny without a surname yet — room for careful research, not invention as fact.

setting_pockets.label: Campti — primary fiction stage
setting_pockets.geography: Unincorporated community, Natchitoches Parish, Louisiana; Cane River National Heritage Area; historically tied to Creole plantations, Catholic parish life, Red River trade and later rail/road.
era_notes: Jim Crow and civil rights as later novel pressure
Present-day Campti as emotional 'home' for returning family story
setting_pockets.mood_for_fiction: Small place where everyone maps everyone else's kin; river memory vs courthouse paper.

setting_pockets.label: Ancient Caddo village (upper Red, Nassonite area)
setting_pockets.geography: Red River above Natchitoches; '500 miles' by river measure in 1805 speech — not modern highway miles. Nassonite / Caddo country per Cole-Jett digest.
setting_pockets.mood_for_fiction: Palisade logic, fifteen soldiers never full, cannon as theatre of sovereignty.

setting_pockets.label: Campti / Campte (deposition home)
setting_pockets.geography: Natchitoches County, New Orleans Territory / early Louisiana; Francois 'of Campte' when sworn.
era_notes: Justice of peace office as paper border
setting_pockets.mood_for_fiction: Old river knowledge translated into sworn English for a new empire.

setting_pockets.label: Yattasee Point / Bayou Pierre (Spanish line)
setting_pockets.geography: SW branch Red ~25 leagues above Natchitoches; later Shreveport area; French grants, Spanish jurisdiction in testimony.
setting_pockets.mood_for_fiction: Border fluidity; who counts as 'French inhabitant' on paper vs ground.

setting_pockets.label: Le Dout / Sabine trading ground
setting_pockets.geography: ~150 miles NW Natchitoches per deposition; Sabine / Lake Fork conjectures (Handbook of Texas).

setting_pockets.label: Claiborne Parish — Jacques branch terminus (1848)
setting_pockets.geography: North Louisiana; Jacques Grappe death Oct 1848 per user batch — not Campti but same story region.
setting_pockets.mood_for_fiction: End-of-life away from birthplace — migration as plot, not failure.

cross_cutting_themes: Campti as moral center of the fiction even when scenes range the upper Red — always ask who gets to come home.
Knowledge of rivers as state asset — Grappe's testimony as cartography of memory.
Company / post / family — blurred institutions on the frontier.
Silence of women and enslaved people in official oaths — narrative space for Rosette and Louise strategies.
Displacement stacks (Yattasee, mill stones, abandoned posts) as emotional pattern for Creole fiction.
US 'legitimacy of land claims' vs oral tenure — pressure on Campti present.
Parallel branches (Jacques/De La Cerda vs Pelagie/Perot) — same Rosette generation, different novels inside one family; Trichel/D'Ortolon ties can appear on the 'wrong' branch first.
